<<<KOT:mythologisation>>>>
{"kind":"cannot-formalise","why":"mythologisation is a process of linguistic/cultural transformation (making something into myth or treating it as mythological). It requires concepts like 'narrative', 'cultural practice', 'interpretation', and 'belief' that are not stably expressible in the prime lexicon and are not anchored in the catalog. The everyday grounding would need anthropological or semiotic primitives outside the scope of this tier."}
<<<END:mythologisation>>>>

{"kind":"molecule","gloss":"a kind of {urn:molecule-v0:body|body} [m] part that is inside the {urn:kernel-v0:inside|inside} of the body; it is not a kind of {urn:kernel-v0:alive|alive} part that makes electrical things happen; many of these parts help other alive parts move and feel; people can see these parts only after very small things are used to make them big.","groundingNote":"a kind of {urn:molecule-v0:body|body} [m] part that is inside the body; it is not a kind of alive part that makes electrical things happen; many of these parts help other alive parts move and feel; people can see these parts only after very small things are used to make them big.","groundingRefs":["urn:molecule-v0:body","urn:kernel-v0:inside","urn:kernel-v0:alive"]}
<<<END:neuroglia>>>>
